summary: mcp-cnes fetches and executes payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: The package's dist/bootstrap.js fetches content from https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket with no publisher attribution — and references process.env in the same flow. Anonymous *.r2.dev buckets are a known payload-distribution pattern (mutable, no version pinning, no hash verification, not associated with the package author's documented infrastructure). The R2 host shape matches infrastructure used in prior npm dropper campaigns (e.g., the @chahuadev cluster). Legitimate Brazilian government health-data SDK functionality (apidadosabertos.saude.gov.br calls in dist/api/dadosabertos.js) does not require fetching code or data from an anonymous third-party bucket. The bootstrap module's name and the fetch+env access combination indicate a load-time payload retrieval path that gives the publisher arbitrary-byte delivery to anyone running this MCP server.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
